 {"id":"brenner_bot-vxp7","title":"Fresh-eyes audit: session-kickoff.ts session initialization","description":"# Goal
Fresh-eyes audit of the session kickoff logic (apps/web/src/lib/session-kickoff.ts), which handles research session initialization.

This file is ~504 lines of session workflow logic that:
- Validates session kickoff form data
- Creates initial session artifacts
- Sets up research thread and initial hypotheses
- Handles operator selection and roster assignment

## Scope
- Review initialization logic for edge cases
- Check form validation completeness
- Verify proper error handling
- Check test coverage
- Fix obvious bugs (no deletions per AGENTS.md)

## Complements prior audits
This pairs with artifact-merge and delta-parser audits, covering the session creation layer.

## Acceptance
- All identified bugs documented or fixed
- Test coverage verified/improved","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonValley","created_at":"2026-01-01T20:52:33.961673556-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-01T20:54:45.862859087-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-01T20:54:45.862859087-05:00","close_reason":"AUDIT COMPLETE: session-kickoff.ts (504 lines) - NO BUGS FOUND. Well-structured role-based session initialization with: (1) Type guards (isAgentRole) for validation, (2) Case-insensitive agent name matching, (3) Keyword fallback heuristics for unknown names, (4) Explicit recipientRoles mapping for adjective+noun Agent Mail identities, (5) Proper error handling for missing/invalid mappings. 12 tests all passing."}

-{"id":"brenner_bot-w5p6","title":"Tutorial Path: Agent-Assisted Research (8 Steps)","description":"# Purpose

Implement the Agent-Assisted tutorial path — 8 steps that teach users how to have their AI coding agent (Claude Code or Codex) study the BrennerBot system and then run the Brenner Loop for them.

This is the **highest-leverage path**. Once an AI agent internalizes the methodology, it can apply it repeatedly to different research questions. The human provides domain expertise and oversight; the AI provides methodological discipline.

---

# The Core Insight

**AI agents can read documentation and internalize methodology.**

When you have Claude Code or Codex:
1. Clone the brenner_bot repo
2. Read AGENTS.md, README.md, and the distillations
3. Explore the operator library and specs

...the agent doesn't just learn commands. It learns to THINK with Brenner's cognitive toolkit.

---

# Tool-Specific Guidance (IMPORTANT)

**Claude Code and Codex have different interfaces.** While the prompts are largely the same, we need:

1. **Claude Code Tab** (for Claude Max users)
   - Uses `claude` command
   - Project context via --project flag
   - Opus 4.5 model

2. **Codex Tab** (for GPT Pro users)
   - Uses `codex` command  
   - Project context works similarly
   - GPT 5.2 model

**Implementation**: Use the same tab component as Quick Start's OS-specific instructions. Show tool-specific setup and verification, but keep the core prompts unified.

---

# Prerequisites

**Required (one of)**:
- Claude Code with Opus 4.5 (Claude Max subscription)
- Codex CLI with GPT 5.2 (GPT Pro subscription)

**Also needed**:
- Basic familiarity with the CLI agent interface
- Git and terminal basics

---

# Step-by-Step Breakdown

## Step 1: Why Agent-Assisted? (~3 min)
Explain the leverage, set expectations, clarify human-AI collaboration model.

## Step 2: Prerequisites (~5 min)
**Tool-specific tabs here**: Verify Claude Code OR Codex installed, check subscription tier.

## Step 3: Clone into Agent Context (~5 min)
Have the agent clone brenner_bot, verify file access.

## Step 4: Agent Studies the System (~10 min) ⭐ KEY STEP
**Uses prompts from Prompt Templates Library** (brenner_bot-u38r)

Agent reads:
- AGENTS.md, README.md
- specs/operator_library_v0.1.md
- At least one distillation

Verification: Agent's summary should mention key operators.

## Step 5: Define Your Research Problem (~5 min)
User provides their question, agent helps refine using Brenner criteria.

## Step 6: Agent Builds the Inputs (~10 min)
Agent searches corpus, composes excerpt, drafts kickoff prompt.
Human checkpoint: User reviews inputs before proceeding.

## Step 7: Agent Runs the Brenner Loop (~10 min)
Agent produces structured artifact with all sections.
Output: ~2000-4000 word research artifact.

## Step 8: Human Review (~5 min)
**Interactive checklist** for reviewing agent output:
- Hypothesis quality checks
- Test quality checks
- Assumption completeness
- Critique substantiveness

Common failure modes documented with fixes.

---

# Critical Dependencies

- brenner_bot-u38r (Prompt Templates Library) - The prompts ARE the interface
- brenner_bot-8i1c (Domain Examples) - Users need to see "good" artifacts to compare

---

# Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] All 8 steps implemented with full content
- [ ] Tool-specific tabs for Claude Code vs Codex
- [ ] Prompts link to Prompt Templates Library
- [ ] Review checklist is interactive
- [ ] Troubleshooting for common agent failures
- [ ] Human oversight emphasized throughout","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-03T14:52:04.355522747-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-04T16:07:24.240563182-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-w5p6","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-do3j","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T14:54:04.502582177-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}

 {"id":"brenner_bot-wcr8","title":"Tutorial Component: TutorialProgress (step indicator sidebar)","description":"# Component: TutorialProgress

The progress indicator that shows users where they are in the tutorial path.

## Behavior

**Desktop (width >= 768px)**:
- Vertical list on left sidebar
- Fixed position, scrolls with page
- Shows all steps with numbers
- Current step highlighted with primary accent
- Completed steps show checkmark + muted color
- Clickable to navigate (if allowed)

**Mobile (width < 768px)**:
- Horizontal progress bar at top
- Minimal text, mostly visual
- "Step X of Y" label
- Collapses to icon on scroll

## Props
\`\`\`typescript
interface TutorialProgressProps {
  steps: Array<{ id: string; title: string; estimatedTime: string }>;
  currentStep: number;
  completedSteps: number[];
  onStepClick?: (stepIndex: number) => void;
  allowJumpAhead?: boolean;
}
\`\`\`

## Tests
- Renders all steps
- Highlights current step
- Shows checkmarks for completed
- Click navigation works when allowed
- Mobile breakpoint layout","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-03T14:54:53.679913239-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-04T13:34:09.443698194-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-04T13:34:09.443698194-05:00","close_reason":"Already implemented: TutorialProgress.tsx exists with 29KB of code including desktop sidebar, mobile dots, swipe gestures, animations, and all specified features","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-wcr8","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-thzu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T14:55:41.743176235-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}

 {"id":"brenner_bot-wpb","title":"Create OperatorSymbol component with tooltip","description":"# Task

Create a distinctive inline component for Brenner operator symbols.

# The Operators

⊘ Level-split | 𝓛 Recode | ≡ Invariant-extract | ✂ Exclusion-test
⟂ Object-transpose | ↑ Amplify | ⊕ Cross-domain | ◊ Paradox-hunt
ΔE Exception-quarantine | ∿ Dephase | † Theory-kill | ⌂ Materialize
🔧 DIY | ⊞ Scale-check

# Design

Each operator symbol should:
1. Stand out from surrounding text (monospace font, slightly larger)
2. Have a subtle background pill
3. Show tooltip on hover with name + definition
4. Link to the operator palette page

# Visual

```
The ⊘ Level-split operator separates...
    ^
    └── pill background, hover shows tooltip
```

# Tooltip Content

- Operator name
- One-line definition
- 'Learn more →' link

# Props

```tsx
interface OperatorSymbolProps {
  symbol: '⊘' | '𝓛' | '≡' | '✂' | '⟂' | '↑' | '⊕' | '◊' | 'ΔE' | '∿' | '†' | '⌂' | '🔧' | '⊞';
  showName?: boolean;  // Show name alongside symbol
  linkToPage?: boolean; // Make it a link to operator page
}
```

# Operator Data

Create a data file with all operator definitions:
```typescript
export const OPERATORS = {
  '⊘': { name: 'Level-split', definition: 'Separate program from interpreter...' },
  // ...
};
```

# Animation

- Subtle scale on hover
- Tooltip fade in

# Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] All 14 operators render correctly
- [ ] Tooltips show on hover
- [ ] Links work if enabled
- [ ] Accessible (keyboard tooltip trigger)
- [ ] Works inline with text","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-29T19:22:13.719833091-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-29T19:29:11.392954222-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-29T19:29:11.392954222-05:00","close_reason":"Folded into brenner_bot-5so.4.5.2 (operator palette UI)","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-wpb","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-8yn","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T19:23:06.878775514-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}
